intended_use: Quantib ND is a non-invasive medical imaging processing application that is intended for automatic labeling, visualization, and volumetric quantification of segmentable brain structures from a set of
intended_use_full: Quantib ND is a non-invasive medical imaging processing application that is intended for automatic labeling, visualization, and volumetric quantification of segmentable brain structures from a set of magnetic resonance (MR) images. The Quantib ND output consists of segmentations, visualizations and volumetric measurements of brain structures and white matter hyperintensities. Volumetric measurements may be compared to reference centile data. It is intended to provide the trained medical professional with complementary information for the evaluation and assessment of MR brain images and to aid the trained medical professional in quantitative reporting.
target_condition: Brain structure assessment and white matter hyperintensity evaluation on MR imaging
ground_truth: Manual segmentations performed by trained raters on the same MR images
other_metrics: Dice index for brain tissue: 0.96 ± 0.01; Dice index for CSF: 0.78 ± 0.05; Dice index for ICV: 0.98 ± 0.00; Dice index for hippocampus: 0.84 ± 0.03; Absolute difference of relative volumes for brain tissue: 1.63 ± 1.06 percentage points; Absolute difference of relative volumes for CSF: 1.67 ± 1.06 percentage points; Dice index for frontal lobe: 0.95 ± 0.01; Dice index for occipital lobe: 0.89 ± 0.03; Dice index for parietal lobe: 0.89 ± 0.03; Dice index for temporal lobe: 0.91 ± 0.02; Dice index for cerebellum: 0.99 ± 0.00; White matter hyperintensities average Dice overlap: 0.61 ± 0.13; White matter hyperintensities absolute difference of relative volumes: 0.2 ± 0.2 percentage points
